questions.q: In which kingdom did the marriage of Rama and Sita take place?
questions.explanation: Rama and Sita were wed at Mithila, the capital of Janaka's kingdom of Videha.

questions.q: Who was the bride that Rama married?
questions.explanation: Rama married Sita, the daughter of King Janaka of Mithila.

questions.q: What did Rama have to do to win Sita's hand in marriage?
options: Lift and string the bow of Shiva
questions.explanation: King Janaka had decreed that whoever could lift and string Shiva's great bow would marry Sita, and Rama broke it.

questions.q: Who was the father of Sita who arranged the marriage?
questions.explanation: King Janaka of Mithila was Sita's father and hosted the bow-contest that decided her marriage.

questions.q: Who was the father of Rama present for the wedding at Mithila?
questions.explanation: King Dasharatha of Ayodhya, Rama's father, traveled to Mithila for the wedding ceremony.

questions.q: According to the card, how many of Dasharatha's sons were married in the grand ceremony?
questions.explanation: All four sons of Dasharatha (Rama and his three brothers) were married together in a single grand ceremony.

questions.q: What happened to Shiva's bow when Rama tried to string it?
questions.explanation: As Rama bent the bow to string it, the mighty weapon snapped in two with a thunderous sound.

questions.q: Which sage escorted Rama and Lakshmana to Mithila before the marriage?
questions.explanation: The sage Vishvamitra brought Rama and Lakshmana to Mithila, where they witnessed the bow and won Sita.

questions.q: Sita is famously described as which kind of daughter to Janaka?
options: An adopted daughter found in a furrow
A daughter sent by the gods in a chariot
His niece raised as a daughter
questions.explanation: Sita was found by Janaka in a furrow while ploughing a field, and so was his adopted, earth-born daughter.

questions.q: The marriage of Rama and Sita is celebrated in tradition as which festival?
questions.explanation: The wedding of Rama and Sita is commemorated as Vivaha Panchami, also known as Sita Vivah.

questions.q: Lakshmana married which sister of Sita?
questions.explanation: Lakshmana was wedded to Urmila, Janaka's own daughter and Sita's sister.

questions.q: Bharata married which bride in the joint ceremony at Mithila?
questions.explanation: Bharata married Mandavi, a daughter of Janaka's brother Kushadhwaja.

questions.q: Shatrughna married which of the brides at the grand wedding?
questions.explanation: Shatrughna married Shrutakirti, another daughter of Kushadhwaja.

questions.q: Mandavi and Shrutakirti, who married Bharata and Shatrughna, were daughters of whom?
questions.explanation: Mandavi and Shrutakirti were the daughters of Kushadhwaja, the brother of King Janaka.

questions.q: Which family priest of Dasharatha presided over the wedding rites at Mithila?
questions.explanation: Sage Vashistha, the royal priest of Ayodhya, officiated the wedding rites alongside Mithila's priests.

questions.q: The original divine bow of Shiva that Rama broke had been entrusted to the ancestors of which king?
questions.explanation: Shiva's bow had been given to Janaka's ancestors (the kings of Mithila) and kept as a heirloom in his court.

questions.q: Who was the chief priest of Janaka's court at Mithila during the marriage?
questions.explanation: Shatananda, son of the sage Gautama and Ahalya, served as Janaka's family priest at Mithila.

questions.q: Which sage and warrior confronted Rama on the road back from Mithila, angered that Shiva's bow was broken?
questions.explanation: Parashurama, devotee of Shiva, intercepted the wedding party and challenged Rama after the bow broke.

questions.q: How is Sita regarded in tradition in relation to the goddess Lakshmi?
questions.explanation: Sita is traditionally venerated as an incarnation of the goddess Lakshmi, consort of Vishnu, whom Rama incarnates.

questions.q: What is the name of the kingdom over which Janaka, Sita's father, ruled?
questions.explanation: Janaka ruled the kingdom of Videha, whose capital was Mithila.

questions.q: What sacred ritual at the heart of a Vedic wedding did Rama and Sita perform together?
questions.explanation: Like all Vedic marriages, Rama and Sita took their vows by walking around the sacred fire (Agni) as witness.

questions.q: Because Janaka famously found Sita while ploughing, she bears which epithet?
questions.explanation: Sita is called Janaki, 'daughter of Janaka,' among her many names including Vaidehi and Maithili.

questions.q: By what name is the great bow of Shiva that Rama broke traditionally known?
questions.explanation: Shiva's bow is called Pinaka, distinguishing it from Vishnu's bow Sharanga that Rama later receives from Parashurama.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, how many men were required just to carry the chest holding Shiva's bow into the assembly?
questions.explanation: Valmiki describes five hundred men drawing the eight-wheeled chest that bore Shiva's immense bow into the court.

questions.q: After Rama broke Shiva's bow, Parashurama challenged him to string which other divine bow?
questions.explanation: Parashurama produced the Vaishnava bow of Vishnu and challenged Rama to bend it, which Rama effortlessly did.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, what did Janaka offer to give along with Sita as the marriage was settled?
options: Vast wealth, cattle, and a dowry of riches
questions.explanation: Janaka bestowed an immense dowry of gold, cattle, horses, and treasures upon the departing brides and grooms.

questions.q: Which queen of Dasharatha was the birth mother of both Lakshmana and Shatrughna?
questions.explanation: Queen Sumitra was the mother of the twins Lakshmana and Shatrughna, who married Urmila and Shrutakirti.

questions.q: In the Valmiki Ramayana, who was sent as a swift envoy from Mithila to Ayodhya to invite Dasharatha to the wedding?
questions.explanation: Janaka dispatched his own messengers to Ayodhya, who rode three days to carry the marriage invitation to Dasharatha.

questions.q: How old is Sita commonly said to be in some traditional reckonings at the time of her marriage to Rama?
questions.explanation: Certain traditional accounts state Sita was about six and Rama somewhat older, though the epic emphasizes the union's sanctity over precise ages.

questions.q: On which auspicious lunar day, celebrated as Vivaha Panchami, is the marriage of Rama and Sita traditionally observed?
options: The fifth day of the bright fortnight of Margashirsha
questions.explanation: Vivaha Panchami falls on the fifth lunar day (panchami) of the bright fortnight of the month of Margashirsha.
